title: contingency-dsl Experiment
description: Experiment and Paper wrapper nodes. An Experiment groups a single-schedule Program or a multi-phase PhaseSequence under a label (e.g., 'Experiment 1'). A Paper groups multiple Experiments as they appear in a single publication.

Experiment.description: A single experiment within a paper. Body is either a Program (single-schedule design) or a PhaseSequence (multi-phase design). Each Experiment has independent annotations — JEAB convention is to describe Subjects and Apparatus independently per experiment.

label.description: Experiment label as it appears in the paper. Examples: 'Experiment 1', 'Experiment 2a'.

Paper.description: A publication containing one or more experiments. Experiments are independent — no annotation sharing between them.

Shaping.description: Syntactic sugar for across-session parameter progressions. Desugars to a sequence of Phase nodes (analogous to Repeat → Tand). Present only in pre-expansion AST; resolved AST contains the expanded Phase list. Reference: Sidman (1960), Tactics of scientific research.

label.description: Base name for generated phases. Expansion appends _1, _2, ..., _n.

steps.description: One or more parameter variables with value lists. All lists must have identical length.

criterion.description: Shared phase-change criterion applied to every generated phase.

schedule_template.description: Schedule expression with {ident} placeholders referencing steps variables.

phase_annotations.description: Annotations shared across all generated phases. May contain {ident} placeholders.

StepsDecl.description: A named parameter variable with a list of literal values for shaping expansion.

name.description: Variable name referenced by {name} placeholders in the schedule template.

values.description: Finite literal list of parameter values. No computation; preserves non-Turing-completeness.

PhaseRef.description: Reference to a previously declared phase's schedule. Resolved during expansion by copying the referenced phase's schedule expression. Forward references are not permitted.

ref.description: Name of the referenced phase. Must match a phase_label declared earlier in the same experiment.
